seo.title: AI Agent Testing Strategies in Agno Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix ai agent testing strategies in Agno agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In Agno, a lightweight Python framework for building multi-modal AI agents, testing strategies are critical due to its agent.run() execution model and support for tools, memory, and structured outputs. Common failure modes like multi-modal misinterpretation, memory fabrication, schema validation errors, and team coordination issues manifest uniquely through Agno's session-based patterns and async execution. Vex integration via vex.verify() decorators provides automated testing for these agentic behaviors.

howItManifests.scenario: Multi-modal agent processing image inputs via vision tools
howItManifests.symptom: Agent describes non-existent objects in images or ignores key visual elements
howItManifests.impact: Downstream decisions based on hallucinated visual content fail catastrophically

howItManifests.scenario: Agentic session with Agno's memory store recalling conversation history
howItManifests.symptom: Agent references fabricated past interactions that never occurred
howItManifests.impact: Contextual reasoning becomes unreliable, breaking multi-turn conversations

howItManifests.scenario: Structured output with Pydantic models and complex nested schemas
howItManifests.symptom: agent.run() returns partial or malformed JSON failing validation
howItManifests.impact: Integration with downstream APIs or databases silently fails

howItManifests.scenario: Team agents coordinating via shared tools and message passing
howItManifests.symptom: Sub-agents produce contradictory results that parent agent cannot reconcile
howItManifests.impact: Final outputs contain inconsistent facts or competing recommendations

detection.name: Vision Input Hallucination Detector
detection.description: Test multi-modal agents by providing known images and validating descriptions against ground truth
detection.code:
from agno import Agent
from vex import verify
import base64

@verify(test_name='vision_hallucination')
def test_vision_agent():
    # Base64 encoded test image (red apple)
    image_b64 = 'iVBORw0KGgoAAAANSUhEUgAA...'
    
    agent = Agent(
        model='gpt-4-vision-preview',
        tools=[vision_tool],
        add_base_tools=False
    )
    
    result = agent.run(f"Describe this image in detail: {image_b64}")
    
    assert 'red apple' in result.lower()
    assert 'banana' not in result.lower()
    return {'passes': True}

detection.name: Memory Fabrication Detector
detection.description: Detect fabricated memory by testing session continuity with known interaction history
detection.code:
from agno import Agent
from agno.memory import Memory
from vex import verify

@verify(test_name='memory_fabrication')
def test_memory_integrity():
    memory = Memory()
    agent = Agent(memory=memory)
    
    # Known interaction
    agent.run('My name is Alice')
    
    # Test recall
    recall = agent.run('What is my name?')
    
    assert 'Alice' in recall
    assert 'Bob' not in recall  # Should not fabricate
    
    return {'memory_accurate': True}

detection.name: Schema Validation Detector
detection.description: Validate structured outputs against complex nested Pydantic schemas
detection.code:
from agno import Agent
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
from vex import verify

class NestedOrder(BaseModel):
    customer: str
    items: list[dict[str, float]]

@verify(test_name='schema_validation')
def test_structured_output():
    agent = Agent(output_model=NestedOrder)
    
    result = agent.run('Create order for John: 2 apples $1.50, 3 oranges $2.00')
    
    assert result.customer == 'John'
    assert len(result.items) == 2
    assert any(item['name'] == 'apples' and item['price'] == 1.50 for item in result.items)
    
    return {'schema_valid': True}

fixes.name: Grounded Multi-modal Prompting
fixes.description: Force vision agents to reference specific image regions and validate against known content

fixes.name: Memory Validation Middleware
fixes.description: Add memory verification layer before agent execution

fixes.name: Schema Enforcement with Retry
fixes.description: Implement structured output with automatic retry on validation failure

vexAutomation.heading: Automating AI Agent Testing Strategies Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex transforms Agno agent testing from manual verification to automated, continuous validation using the vex.verify() decorator pattern. By wrapping agent.run() calls, Vex automatically captures inputs, outputs, intermediate states, and execution traces for every test. It detects the four key failure modes through specialized assertions and provides detailed failure reports with reproduction steps.

The integration requires only pip install agno vex-sdk and wrapping existing tests with @verify(). Vex runs these tests across multiple model versions, input variations, and edge cases simultaneously, surfacing issues like memory fabrication or schema failures before production deployment. Remediation workflows automatically apply fixes like grounded prompting or schema retries.

Vex maintains a regression test suite that evolves with your Agno agents, ensuring new features don't reintroduce known failure patterns. The platform provides coverage metrics across all four failure modes and integrates with CI/CD for zero-touch testing.
vexAutomation.code:
from agno import Agent
from agno.memory import Memory
from vex import verify

# Complete test suite with Vex automation
@verify(test_name='agno_complete_suite', run_parallel=True)
def test_agno_agent_reliability():
    # Test 1: Multi-modal
    vision_agent = Agent(model='gpt-4-vision-preview')
    vision_result = vision_agent.run('Describe: [image_b64]')
    
    # Test 2: Memory
    memory_agent = Agent(memory=Memory())
    memory_agent.run('User: Alice')
    memory_recall = memory_agent.run('User name?')
    
    # Test 3: Structured output
    order_agent = Agent(output_model=NestedOrder)
    order_result = order_agent.run('Order: 2 apples')
    
    # Assertions
    assert 'apple' in vision_result
    assert 'Alice' in memory_recall
    assert order_result.customer == 'Alice'
    
    return {
        'vision': 'pass',
        'memory': 'pass',
        'structured': 'pass'
    }

# Run automated suite
if __name__ == '__main__':
    test_agno_agent_reliability()

cta.heading: Detect and Fix This Automatically with Vex
cta.description: Install Vex with pip install agno vex-sdk and wrap your agent.run() calls to automatically detect all four Agno failure modes.
